hero.name: Gustavo Viana
hero.title: Software Engineer & Security Researcher
hero.description: I build secure systems at the intersection of AI and offensive security. Independent researcher and engineer focused on real-world impact.

about.label: About
about.title: Who I am
about.body_1: I'm a Software Engineering student at Anhanguera Educacional, graduating in January 2029. From early on I developed a deep interest in cybersecurity — not just as a career, but as a way of thinking about systems, failure modes and trust.
about.body_2: My work moves between applied software engineering and security research. I build tools that solve real problems: from phishing detection and malicious URL analysis to enterprise attack simulations and government infrastructure assessments.
about.body_3: I have a growing focus on AI Security — specifically how large language models can be manipulated and how to build practical defenses against it. My most recent research proposes and empirically validates the SPEF Framework, a four-layer architecture for securing LLM-based systems.
about.body_4: I believe high-level security requires both technical depth and clarity of communication. That's why I also write: articles, analyses and technical content that make complex topics accessible without losing rigor.

projects.label: Projects
projects.title: What I build
projects.subtitle: Projects that solve real problems at the intersection of security, engineering and user experience.

items.name: PhishGuard
items.description: A phishing detection tool that analyzes URLs and returns a 0–100 risk score based on multiple factors: Shannon entropy, WHOIS data, suspicious TLDs, malicious keywords and HTTPS absence. Responsive interface with dark mode and session history.
items.problem: Malicious URLs are the primary entry point for phishing attacks. Without an accessible rapid analysis tool, users and security teams rely on intuition to identify threats.

items.name: Hermes Links
items.description: A full-stack URL shortening engine with custom Base62 encoding, real-time analytics and trilingual support (pt-BR, EN, ES). Dashboard with most-traveled paths and elegant error handling.
items.problem: Long URLs are hard to share, track and analyze. Hermes solves this with a fast, scalable and internationalized system — demonstrating well-architected software engineering.

items.name: Número Seguro
items.description: A platform for validating and analyzing phone numbers with a focus on fraud prevention. Identifies suspicious patterns, classifies numbers by risk level and enhances security in digital interactions.
items.problem: Fake or suspicious phone numbers are widely used in scams and digital fraud. Número Seguro provides an accessible verification layer for users and systems.

research.label: Research
research.title: Publications
research.subtitle: Independent academic research in software engineering with a focus on applied security.

items.title: Secure Prompt Engineering: A Practical Framework for Mitigating Prompt Injection and Data Leakage in LLM-based Systems
items.abstract: This paper proposes and empirically validates SPEF (Secure Prompt Engineering Framework), a four-layer defensive architecture for LLM-based systems. A controlled experiment with Llama-3.3-70B demonstrates an 86.4% reduction in adversarial attack success rate — from 17.6% to 2.4%.

items.title: Security Posture Assessment of Brazilian Municipal Web Portals
items.abstract: A comprehensive security analysis of 27 Brazilian municipal portals (26 state capitals + Federal District), evaluating SSL/TLS configurations, security headers implementation and service exposure patterns. Only 22.2% implement HSTS; 44.4% operate with suboptimal SSL/TLS.

blog.label: Newsletter
blog.title: Newsletter
blog.subtitle: Security news, analyses and reflections on AI and software engineering — curated and commented.
blog.no_posts: Coming soon. First issues on the way.

books.label: Reading
books.title: Books I recommend
books.subtitle: Works that shaped how I think about security, systems and technology.

items.title: Hacking: The Art of Exploitation
items.author: Jon Erickson
items.description: The most honest book on hacking that exists. Erickson doesn't teach you tools — he teaches you to think like an attacker at the level of memory, shellcode and buffer overflow exploitation. Required reading for anyone who wants to understand security for real, not just run scripts.

items.title: Black Hat Python
items.author: Justin Seitz & Tim Arnold
items.description: Python applied to the offensive side of security: building pentest tools, network sniffers, trojans, fuzzers and vulnerability exploitation. Direct, practical and no-nonsense. The second edition is fully updated for Python 3.

items.title: The Web Application Hacker's Handbook
items.author: Dafydd Stuttard & Marcus Pinto
items.description: The bible of web application security. Covers all relevant attack vectors — SQL Injection, XSS, CSRF, broken access control, business logic flaws — with real technical depth. Even though it's from 2011, the fundamentals remain valid and are still taught in OWASP courses.

contact.label: Contact
contact.title: Let's talk
contact.subtitle: I'm open to conversations about security, AI, collaborative projects or opportunities.
